## reference · web form design labels structure

* Keep form labels close to their fields, ideally immediately above or to the left.
* Use a single column layout for fields to maintain user flow.
* Group related fields and labels together with clear visual separation.
* Use logical sequencing for fields and value choices.
* Avoid ambiguous spacing between labels and fields.
* Include label attributes for screen readers.

* Avoid using placeholder text as labels.
* Match field size to expected input length.
* Clearly distinguish optional and required fields.
* Explain input and formatting requirements.

* Avoid "Reset" and "Clear" buttons; use "Cancel" buttons with caution.
* Provide highly visible and specific error messages.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/web-form-design/
